name: Salted Herbs
recipeInstructions:
These seasonings are commonly used in traditional French-Canadian recipes. "Herbs preserved with vegetables and salt make a lively seasoning for soups, particularly pea soup, sauces, stews, and omelettes. A commercial brand, Les Herbes Salees du bas du fleuve, is marketed by J.Y. Roy of St. Flavie, Quebec. This recipe comes from the Metis district."
1. In a large bowl, combine herbs and vegetables.
2. Layer 1 inch of herb mixture in the bottom of a crock or glass bowl.
3. Sprinkle with some salt.
4. Repeat layers until all of the herb mixture and salt is used.
5. Cover and refrigerate for 2 weeks.
6. Drain off accumulated liquid.
7. Pack herb mixture into sterilized jars.
8. Refrigerate until ready to use.
Makes about 5 to 6 cups.
